Recent papers

Recent papers on the n^2+1 prime conjecture, surfaced by the prominence of their authors in this directory.

Showing 15 papers, ranked by the Top 100 standing of their authors then by recency. Authors who appear in the Top 100 are shown in bold.

#TitleAuthorsPublishedBest rank
1Skolem Meets Bateman-HornFlorian Luca, James Maynard, Armand Noubissie, Joël Ouaknine, James Worrell2023-08#2
2Primitive root bias for twin primes II: Schinzel-type theorems for totient quotients and the sum-of-divisors functionStephan Ramon Garcia, Florian Luca, Kye Shi, Gabe Udell2019-06#2
3Primitive root biases for prime pairs I: existence and non-totality of biasesStephan Ramon Garcia, Florian Luca, Timothy Schaaff2017-08#2
4Primitive root bias for twin primesStephan Ramon Garcia, Elvis Kahoro, Florian Luca2017-05#2
5Heuristics of the Cocks-Pinch methodMin Sha2012-11#6
6Möbius cancellation on polynomial sequences and the quadratic Bateman-Horn conjecture over function fieldsWill Sawin, Mark Shusterman2020-08#13
7Bateman-Horn, polynomial Chowla and the Hasse principle with probability 1Tim Browning, Efthymios Sofos, Joni Teräväinen2022-12#18
8Schinzel Hypothesis on average and rational pointsAlexei N. Skorobogatov, Efthymios Sofos2020-05#18
9Cyclotomic polynomials with prescribed height and prime number theoryAlexandre Kosyak, Pieter Moree, Efthymios Sofos, Bin Zhang2019-10#18
10Rational points and prime values of polynomials in moderately many variablesKevin Destagnol, Efthymios Sofos2018-01#18
11Serre's problem on the density of isotropic fibres in conic bundlesEfthymios Sofos2016-02#18
12Estimating the tail of the singular product in the multivariate Bateman-Horn conjectureVictor Volfson2026-04#23
13Probabilistic analytical approach to determining the asymptotics of prime objects on the initial interval of the natural seriesVictor Volfson2015-06#23
14Contradictions in some primes conjecturesVictor Volfson2014-07#23
15Counting conjugacy classes of subgroups of ${\rm PSL}_2(p)$Gareth A. Jones2024-11#25

Most cited (all time)

The ten most-cited Mathematics works on the n^2+1 prime conjecture, by citation count in OpenAlex. Results are filtered to the Mathematics field (OpenAlex field 26) and ranked by total citations.

The Top 100 ranking is designed to capture prominent, currently active researchers. If an author in this table is not linked to a profile, it is usually because they are deceased or less active than the researchers in the ranking, not because they are less significant to the field.

#TitleAuthorsYearCitations
1Almost-primes represented by quadratic polynomialsHenryk Iwaniec1978136
2Primes represented by quadratic polynomials in two variablesHenryk Iwaniec197454
3Mean values of Dirichlet polynomials and applications to linear equations with prime variablesStephen Choi; Angel Kumchev200637
4A Report on Primes of the Form k⋅2 n + 1 and On Factors of Fermat NumbersRaphael M. Robinson195832
5Primes represented by irreducible polynomials in one variablePaul T. Bateman; Roger A. Horn196531
6New quadratic polynomials with high densities of prime valuesMichael J. Jacobson; Hugh C. Williams200229
7Primes are nonnegative values of a polynomial in 10 variablesYu. V. Matijasevič198127
8On prime valued polynomials and class numbers of real quadratic fieldsR. A. Mollin; Hywel C Williams198827
9Polynomial values free of large prime factorsCécile Dartyge; Gérald Tenenbaum; Greg Martin200224
10Quadratic polynomials which have a high density of prime valuesGilbert Fung; Hywel C Williams199024

Maintained by Steve Hubbard (admin@wwin2p1.org). Data sources: arXiv, OpenAlex, zbMATH Open, Mathematics Genealogy Project.
